intro: Tragium dichotomum, also known as Wild Carrot or Carrot Weed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Apiaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and divided leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antispasmodic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Tragium dichotomum include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, such as carotenoids and luteolin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to treat a range of health issues, and is also used as a flavoring agent in some foods and beverages.
